reviews.review_text:
I just found out the current price of this game and now I have to review it. NEW PEOPLE DO NOT BUY THIS GAME AT IT'S CURRENT PRICE. NO. DO NOT. GO BACK. 

I have 1,000 hours in this janky game. I play 7 days to Die as someone who just enjoys building things, and the zombies add a kind of fun element to your typical survival game. The ability to mod 7 days to Die is honestly one of the best things about it if you're coming into this space looking for a survival/crafting game with a zombie spin. 

However, having played Ark, Conan, Nightingale, Enshrouded, Sims 4, Raft, Green Hell, soooo many other survival crafters, I can say with 100% certainty THIS GAME IS NOT WORTH 45 DOLLARS. LOL WUT ARE THE FUN PIMPS EVEN ON. CONAN EXILES just upgraded to unreal engine 5, and is priced at 39 dollars. ENSHROUDED IS $30 dollars. ENSHROUDED YA'LL. THAT GAME IS BEAUTIFUL, HAS AN INCREDIBLE BUILD SYSTEM AND PROGRESSION. 

7 days to Die is IS NOT WORTH MORE THAN CONAN EXILES OR ENSHROUDED I PROMISE YOU. IT'S RIDCULOUS THIS GAME IS PRICED AT 45 DOLLARS. SHAME ON YOU, FKN GREEDY AZZ FUN PIMPS. 

New people looking to play 7 days, do not be fooled by the review rating into thinking this game is worth the price of entry. IT IS NOT. IT IS NOT WORTH IT. You were not here for the deep lore surrounding this game, you do not know what a mess it has been, you were not here when the games steam rating tanked, you do not know the fight the fans have had with the Fun Pimps who clearly hate their fan base. TRUST ME. I still play this stupid game, but I got it for like $7 dollars in the middle of all the alphas. DO NOT PAY 45 FOR THIS GAME. NO. NO.

BUILDERS ESPEICALLY. MY GOD. This building system is so damn janky and I played old school ARK. Every. Single Building piece no matter the shape takes up 4 squares. Oh you want to put down a cup on a table? FOUR SQUARES. You want to put down a FLAT ROOF CEILING PIECE? FOUR SQUARES. You know what makes this game fun? Yes yes, I know. It's Voxel. Well there are plenty of other games that are Voxel and the shape of the building piece is actually the shape it takes up. Not 7 days! No matter the shape EVERYTHING takes up the same space as CUBE. WTF. 

THE MODDERS. THE MODDERS ARE WHAT MAKES THIS GAME FUN. PAY THEM 45 DOLLARS DO NOT PAY THE FUN PIMPS. 

If this game goes on sale for like $10 buy it. Anything else? Nope.

Not that anyone that likes zombie games hasn't already played this one, but it's one of the best out there.

Some mods that make it even better (as of May 2026 anyway)
Darkness Falls (if they ever update it)
Izy-All In One gun Pack
Hybrid Learn by Use
Bdub's Vehicles
ZZTong's POIs
Craft from Storage
Advanced Weapons
Better Stacks
Project Z
Ramos Zombie Tracker
The Mean One Wandering Hordes Plus

7 Days to Die has completely destroyed the survival fantasy I used to have about myself. I thought if society collapsed, I would be resourceful, tough, maybe even the respected leader of a small group of survivors. Turns out I would spend the apocalypse rummaging through kitchen cabinets for canned chili, eating boiled meat in a shack made of crooked wooden blocks, and getting mauled to death because I forgot to reload my pipe shotgun. Every seven days, the game gives me plenty of warning that an army of zombies is coming, and every seven days I somehow act shocked when they rip through my “impenetrable fortress,” which is usually just a refurbished gas station held together by cobblestone, panic, and one door I forgot to upgrade. I do not survive the blood moon. I simply inconvenience it for a few minutes before sprinting into the darkness wearing plant fiber pants and regret.

The humiliation does not stop when the zombies leave. I have broken into abandoned houses with the confidence of a trained scavenger, only to immediately step on broken glass, fall through the ceiling, wake up six sleepers, and die next to a toilet I was trying to search for a pistol. I collect garbage like it is treasure, I treat a working wrench like a religious artifact, and I have been emotionally outsmarted by zombies who cannot even use doors. The game says I have seven days to prepare, but what it really means is I have seven days to gather resources, build a base, make a plan, ignore that plan entirely, and die somewhere stupid while carrying everything valuable I own. Rated 3 half-eaten cans of sham out of 5 — I would not last one week in the apocalypse, but I will absolutely continue pretending I might.

Fun game, absolutely not worth $45. Devs are out of their minds. Recommend on sale for anything less than $20. Asking for more on a game that's underbaked and dated since before full release is pure greed.

sorry but legit got out of my way to not recommend this dissapointing mutating garbage. im tired of dumb ass mechanics that come and go over the years. this isnt some ex relationship type ♥♥♥♥ bro make up your mind how this game evolves and what you want it to be. drinking smoothies to cancel out elemental biome hazards isnt some revolutionary gaming mechanic. maybe add swimming cause its been almost 14 ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ years of just sliding through water molecules. no one likes the pointless modifications that drag out the grinding just for the sake of stretching the gameplay. at this point just add rebirths

One of the best games I have ever played , You need to try it to see for yourself , the game is not too harsh on the Pc or laptop which are low end or "bad " , Before you know it , you are playing for few hours in a row!
They have updated, and thusly ruined this game at each and every turn that the only way its worth playing is modded. And selling it to the DBD people doesn't exactly fill me with hope for the future.

It’s a great game if you like exploring, action, grinding, and RPGs. It offers a variety of builds, weapons, and skills to choose from, but it is not recommended if you are afraid of jump scares. One challenging aspect of this game is that zombies attack you every week. If these zombie attacks are too stressful, I recommend changing the interval to every 20 days or more, or reducing the number of zombies that spawn during the attack.

You gotta commend the Fun Pimps, they're really dedicated to making their game worse with every update! I've never seen someone do it with such passion and vigor, it's frankly inspiring.

Associating with Behavior Interactive is truly the cherry on top. Now we can have two sets of clueless people behind the wheel for the price of one.

I'll save the AI art for dessert.
Could be better. Better graphics, better cosmetics, better zombies better quests, scenerio etc. Not bad but not good. It's somewhere in the middle.

I LOVE this game but i paid 20 dollars for it 45 bucks is criminal for a game as old as it is i cannot recommend a game like that 
other than that this game is amazing i been playing it for like a decade
if it goes on sale for under 20 bucks then i say get it but no more

I used to love this game, but I can't honestly recommend it anymore because The Fun Pimps or as I liked to call them The No Fun Pimps like to go out of their way to ruin any type of fun you discover. It's almost like a social experiment and people keep falling for it. You the player find a cool way to play, whether it is some new type of base design or build idea and what do The No Fun Pimps do? Nerf the sh*t out of it or just make it so the game itself tries to kill itself... The game is a tower defense game at its core and somehow they even attacked that... logic! Imagine if Nintendo looked at Mario Kart and said "Let's get rid of the cars" how fckn stupid can you be. Ever since the update where they decided that zombies needed an I.Q. to put Albert Einstein and Steven Hawking on the short bus the game has lost all its joy to me. How does a brain dead zombie that can't even wipe its own ass look at my fckn base and know instantly where to attack it to bring the whole thing down? This is no longer a good game and I can't recommend it because it's not the game that I fell in love with...

At 45 bucks.... nah. I wanted to play with my friend but i got this game when it was dirt cheap. No clue why it's so expensive now. Mostly for the mods I saw on Youtube. The general game is like a 15 dollar quality.
Not worth the price especially when the devs consistently make the game worse every patch and punish player creativity. Great potential ruined by a terrible attitude and incompetence from the devs.

How am I supposed to get my friends to buy this game when it's a janky zombie game thats years old priced at 45$ and looks like hotdog water. This is not worth 45$. Lower the game back down to like 10 - 15$ where it should be and people will come back. I used to be a big fan of this game and its development but it's like the pot of greed spawned in the Fun Pimpz office and robbed them of all logic. Weird game designs, story telling so bad the fans have to come up with ideas, mods that outclass the game designers by people who dont even get paid... Everywhere you turn there is something apparent. Lazy and greedy devs doing near the bare minimum just to keep people buying instead of actually listening to fans. I hope something changes

Early Access release 2013
Steam release 2024

still no facial animations even simple ones would be better on traders,
 they have that sentient wax creature vibe,
 also no bandits so it feels pretty empty in certain areas when you're driving,

for full price i'd be pretty depressed but i bought a 2PACK for $18 in 2017 and at that price point, its a solid game. $58 CAD is absolute coconuts though maybe less money to actors frolicking in the snow and more to the boys that do animations

Not worth $40; the game will rot in your Steam library the moment you figure out how the AI works and friends get bored of the game. This is coming from someone who has loved the game for a very long time. If you want a good zombie game, try Project Zomboid.

It's buggy, it's progressively getting worse, it's got microtransactions, it's gameplay loop is dull, it's progression is tedious

...but at least it's free. Wait, nevermind, no it's not.

From all of the Survival Zombie games, this might be the longest living worst one, it's a grind, zombies are bullet sponges, it has artificial progression blocks, it looks like a mix of a Unity game with a mobile game and Zombies AI during sieges are literally one of the most unintuitive unpredictable things I've ever experienced.

Given the state of the game and development over the decades I can only deduce that the reviews have been botted and are not organic.
Longtime zombie survival fan, and needed to scratch that Minecraft itch... This game intertwines both, and does it exceedingly well. IM ADDICTED!
Actually surviving is a pain in the ass. Its frankly easier to keep dying to progress the game than to actually try to survival.

I really tried to like this game, but it sucks. Spent a lot of time adding mods to fix and add a lot of things vanilla should have, but it's just too easy to ♥♥♥♥ up your saves or end up having cpu bottlenecks which make it perform like crap. Sound design is just atrocious, like everything was recorded inside a dirty roadside gas station bathroom. 
The only good thing this game has is its community. Most people are helpful and nice.

I just can't enjoy the game with the AI as it is. The new zombies and their pathing greatly encourage "meta" bases built to cheese the AI and anything other than cheese bases get shredded. Frankly, base building was far more fun in some of the earlier builds with simpler AI, no jiggle physics for zombies (please don't sexualize the dead, its gross), and before some of the dumber enemies like demolishers.

I’ve got a love/hate relationship with 7 Days to Die. Every time I come back to it, the early game pulls me right back in. The start is fast-paced, tense, and genuinely addictive, very similar to games like Conan Exiles where the survival grind at the beginning feels rewarding and exciting. Scavenging, building up from nothing, and preparing for the first blood moon never gets old.

My biggest issue, and honestly the reason I keep quitting and returning, is the constant focus on upgrading existing mobs instead of adding meaningful new content. I’d love to see more unique enemy types, new regions, underground areas, ruined cities, or dangerous endgame zones designed to challenge overpowered bases in creative ways. Right now it can feel like the solution is just “make zombies stronger,” rather than evolving the world itself.

The pathfinding is also a killer sometimes. Watching zombies exploit weird AI routes or tunnel through structures in awkward ways can take away from the fun of building elaborate defences. Base building is one of the game’s strongest features, so when pathfinding feels off, it really stands out.

That said, I still keep coming back because the core gameplay loop is fantastic. Few survival games nail that “starting from absolutely nothing” feeling as well as this one does.

Dev keeps changing the game. At this time, the only thing that is unchanged is the controls.
P.S. ts looks AI generated

Wait for sale, not worth $45. Could be a top 10 game of all time for many if you have a group. But for what the game is, $45 is laughable considering how deaf the developers are.

I was lucky and got to play with a group of 5-8 friends, not sure if it'd ever been worth it playing with randoms or solo. Bought the game for like $5 ten years ago and waited to play it until I had a large group.

Modding scene is great (or was when I last played). However, devman is ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ stupid and not only will not listen to the player base, they will make sure to ♥♥♥♥ over players by making certain changes impossible through modding in order to protect their "ViZUn" (vision). Unless something has changed, it's impossible to adjust zombie AI to make them act like they have in past versions of the game, particularly that you can't disable them from automagically knowing where you are 24/7 during a blood moon. Because you know what's more realistic than an underground doomsday bunker on a Blood Moon night? A tiny ass tight rope going to your ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ treehouse. Care to know which one is a better defense in this game? I'll give you a hint: it ain't a bunker capable of surviving nuclear armageddon--because that'd make too much ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ sense.

I personally like the game, but will only play it under very specific conditions and can only recommend it to people with the same caveats (play with a group, I feel like game is bland without mods, abandon your underground bunker dreams)
From how it looks to how it plays, this feels like a mobile game that got ported to Steam. In 2026, I don't see how you could get anything worthwhile from this, it just feels and looks like trash.

Fantastic game, it has kept us busy for a lot of hours. I will definitely jump back in sometime in the future. It is worth all the money. Don't tell the developers of course, they could totally justify charging more for this game.

My favorite cozy zombie-killing game. With the occasional fight for your life as dozens of zombies come for your blood.
There are so many customization options (with more on the way at the time of this writing) that you can craft pretty much whatever experience you want and challenge yourself - or not. It's basically high-poly Minecraft with optional horde nights.

love the game. have been playing since it was a free release. Im really happy that youve added the ability to keep bees and collect honey, but I really wish you would add the ablilty to raise chickens and collect eggs. the further you get in the game the harder eggs are to collect

This is the only good survival game, even tho thats only because im playing with my friends.

The Good

1.Buildings
There is so many buildings, on my like 4 worlds ive played, ive only seen the same building twice. 
Besides that all of the buildings are like mini levels, you go through one side, exit out the other, and come out hopefully with a lot of loot. Because blocks are strong and you most likely miss out on loot by building around zombies

This is ofc ignoring pipes you can turn on and off, keys and buttons that unlock doors.

2. Zombies
While there is few, there is enough variety to not feel stale. 
There is Light zombies, Normal zombies, And Heavy Zombies (they are fat)
They all get knocked down at half health, and you can tear of their limbs with knives and other pointy things, with the 3 classes of zombies having less or more health.

They can also be feral zombies that have a lot of health (dependent on class), always run, and drop more loot. And those are the "base" zombies, it changes based on biome, like the burned forest having ferals on fire and the snow forest having rock throwing zombies.

This is ignoring a few stinkers we will talk about later.

3. Weapons
Weapons are pretty good, you start with some really bad weapons, that just barely deal more damage then your fists, before reading magazines and getting guns, hammers, knifes, ect that can one shot some of the most dangerous zombies.

Before a few hours into the game (if you are not exp or unlucky) you cant make bullets or any good ammo so you have to be conservative with it.

All weapons can head shot, which can some times one shot a feral if you have high level gear. Some times the zombies cover their face while walking and it can be hard to head shot them, but its not that bad

4. Exploring
Its a drag at first, with low stats and weak weapons you always loot near you base, which assuming you not cheating with a horde base, could have awful loot. After getting your first bike or minibike, you can explore into other biomes

5. Horde night
Again assuming your not cheating, horde night will really beat your ass if you are unprepared. Even then its quite difficult, assuming you have seen the sun in the past 2 days you wont hit all of you shots, and with so many zombies you WILL lose a lot of bullets.  So sometimes you have to go down to get more bullets or shoot them with weaker guns that have bullets.

I dont know if its just me and my friend but we tend to get some zombies raiding our base, which started around day 18-19, its more of an annoyance then a challenge, we have gotten far enough to deal with ferals easily

The Bad

1. Zombies are sometimes bs
The vulture zombie is a pain in the ass, its just there to deal like 4 damage and block you camera for 2 seconds. Adds nothing to the game need a total rework imo

The dog deal a lot of damage and have a lot more health then you would think.

2. The trader can spawn 1km away at times, and even away from buildings, making it takes 14 million years to get to him. Andi dont want to cheat or get the thousands of resources to make a small base near him, id rather clear out a small gas station and base there.

3. Water 
In one of the newer updates you can now get water from jars, or maybe its old i took a year break from this game, but sometimes you dont get the jars back. Like what happened? Did it shatter when it drunk out of it?

4. Zombie given effects
The zombies sometimes give you effects like bruises, bleeding and most often, brain damage. This always gives you infection no matter what but the brain damage slows you to a crawl, its too short to cure it with pills and being unarmed for that long in the first place will really hurt you or just kill you


The Conclusion
Its a pretty good game, its a pain at times but most of that can be ignored by not doing those things,
Some times its bs but doesnt really matter.
Definitely my favorite co-op survival game that I've played. The grind is rewarding and base building techniques are always fun to test out.

7 Days to Die has got me HOOKED! I've played lots of Survival and Zombie games, but this one has so much going for it: Great design, graphics, characters, sense of tension, danger, and accomplishment, intense fighting, great building and crafting options, tons of varied strategy, exploration, and the gift of retaining all your spoils and regaining health after being killed! Sometimes the path forward or details like Repair are confusing, but experimenting or searching works it all out. Bravo, developers. A game to love by people who love gaming!

The game does a great job as a survival simulator. I haven't put a huge number of hours into it yet, so I can't fully judge every aspect of the game, but I can already say that the graphics, movement, and overall atmosphere are very enjoyable.

What impressed me the most is how easy it is to get immersed in the experience. It's a great way to take your mind off everyday problems and have a good time, whether you're playing alone or with friends.

Just keep in mind that the game is still in development, so some features and mechanics may change over time. Overall, my experience has been very positive so far.

Great game. I  keep finding myself comming back to this game over and over. Just a nice game where I can build and have the jump scares I need to keep entertained. Though I do wish they would give a self sort to storage and allow work benches to pull from the storage boxes.
This game is great.. that said the Fun Pimps are a bunch of pretentious developers who think they're better then the community that plays their game...
Great survival features and mechanics.  Difficulty easily managed to suit playstyles.
if u are about to get this game. make sure u like sudden jump scares and to spend hours building a base to watch it crumble in a last stand, using every last bullet.
Great game, One of my favorite zombie games to play, Highly recommend
Fantastic game — besides the amazing horror atmosphere, this game has the most enjoyable crafting mechanics of any crafting game I've played.
It's fun to explore the open world and the survival aspect is less tedious than I expected. It's actually kind of fun to have to keep up your food and water intake. It's fun building bases.

Zombie game nom nom on the nom... 
Every seventh dawn, they come—
a tide of teeth and empty eyes,
and I stand between the living
and the hunger that never dies.
The moon keeps count,
the dead keep marching,
and I keep breathing
one week at a time.

Amazing game. I have about 9k hours in rust as well so its nice to see how much time the devs have really put in to make the crafting and base building a personal and fun thing. If you "beat" the normal game and find yourself getting a little bored of it check out "darkness falls" it has a lot more gear and guns to pick from, you can make sick hot rods to drive around. Not to ruin the end game but you can go into a area 51 type bunker and yeahhh no more spoilers! amazing games both of them. keep up the great work!

Wow this gamed has changed in the years since it first came on the scene. I remember originally I thought of it as Minecraft Zombies. But they have improved the game so much I have a hard time even believing it is the same game. Without going into too much detail, it is a blast to play - especially with friends. Building bases and defending against hoards is a laugh-out-loud shooting frenzy. Watching traps going off and automatic defense guns firing can get the adrenaline flowing. If you haven't played this in a while, give it a shot. And if you've never played it, well, you are in for a treat.

Boy this was a hard review. I have about 1,000 hours into this but most of my love comes from the fact that there really isn't any other game like it. Back in the days it was a breakthrough, but every single update - [b]EVERY[/b] single update - makes the game worse. It's still a fun game but man the devs have given up entirely. 

Be lucky to get a major update each year and each update seems to make the game worse. It's impossible to log how many times they have removed a feature only to add it back at a later date when they realised how much players enjoyed it. They just don't seem to understand what makes their game fun. 

Regardless it's still fun. As soon as another game crops up that has similar mechanics, this will be dead though.
Amazing game that gets better & better. I recommend downloading the bandit/NPC & fishing/Boating Mods

The Ultimate Zombie Sandbox 

After nearly 500 hours and hunting down every last achievement, I can confidently say that 7 Days to Die is one of the most addictive, rewarding survival experiences on the market. It perfectly blends genres in a way few other games have ever successfully pulled off. I have played the game vanilla with no mods as the customization options are solid enough. Base building is fun and strategic and really gives you a sense of accomplishment when you make a base that survives day 7. The updates the devs have put out have been good. (they brought back jars!) And not only that the developers haven't just patched bugs; they have completely overhauled graphics, reworked the weather and rendering engines, redesigned zombie AI, and continuously added fresh POIs. Whether you are playing solo as a survivalist, or running a chaotic server with friends, the value for money here is insane. Getting 100% achievements was a grueling, brilliant ride. If you love crafting, survival, and tower defense, do yourself a favor and pick this up. Many laughs and memories were made on this game and I can definitely say I am looking forward to what The Fun pimps have planned for the future. If I had to give this game a con its simply the price, however this game goes on sale for a price that is hard to pass up.

Ive been playing 7D2D for years now. 1st on xbox, then on pc. The devs are not the best, they have the mindset players should play their game, but only the way the devs want, so it creates conflict. The game has gone through multiple major shifts, but overall, its really a great game. Im not gonna say its perfect, but its well worth the money, just understand this game is probably going to always be in early access. I give the game a strong 7.5/10

ซื้อมาเมื่อ 2 ปีก่อนในราคา 175 กว่าบาทเพราะตอนนั้นยังอยู่ในขั้น Alpha อยู่ รู้จักเกมนี้จากพี่หมิว MT-Mew หลังจากเล่นๆมานานคิดว่าค่อนข้างสนุกอยู่นะ เล่นกับเพื่อนได้ด้วย โซโลออฟไลน์ก็ยังได้เคยเล่นตอนเน็ตดับ อาจมีติดตรงที่ต้องใช้เวลาเล่นค่อนข้างเยอะเพื่อหาเงินหาของดีๆ แต่ระหว่างทางก็สนุกดีนะ

this is a really fun game to play solo or with people. this is the only game that when i play i lose track of time. i think everyone should at least try it. but there are many problems with this game too. for being a 13 year old game, it doesnt run very well even with all settings set low. and the multiplayer severs lag a lot. but every game has problems, and thats ok. its still a very fun game.
The game is a lot of fun, the developers are adding more stuff. Just wish they would put more into the game. Overall with or without mods it is a good game

One of my favorite survival meets Minecraft style games. I play it solo on my own time and also join my friends server a few times a week with his friends and family. I would recommend this game if you guys love the thrill of building up your stats, and getting stronger skills as well.

I enjoy the game. I like the early scramble and the rush to mid-game. Late game is a little weak and it's less about exploration than a math problem of how to handle x group with least amount of non-renewable resources that I brought. The modding scene is great, so after you get bored with the base game, therre's still plenty of life left.

I would recommend this game to others because of its replayability, and it is generally a fun experience. However, it does have its fair share of bugs and issues, like me and my friends being unable to join each others games with "EAC" issues or it just giving us a join failed or disconnected prompt.
one of the best survival games i have played my daughter and i love it. We play on pc and ps5 together and its been a blast

Me parece un muy buen juego, creo que debería mejorar la experiencia endgame y misiones mas variadas...
Aún así lo recomiendo.
Great game for scavenging, building and fighting.
great game if you enjoy a build/survival type of game. if new player highly suggest to start on lower difficulty to avoid rage quitting.
The game is kinda fun but i would guess it is more of a friend group game. After day 21 game becomes kind of boring, a little grindy, sometimes a little hard.

I love survival games, and this game has come a long way, and to me been fun through all of its different iterations. My only complaint is not really on Fun Pimps, and more so my own problem. I wash adding mods was a little easier like it can be with games like Ark. I recommend.
cross-platform, zombies, multiplayer, survival, realistic
This game is really fun, has some neat story details that are more environmental than anything, but it is pretty unoptimized, it is still a really great game despite that though

One of THE best games i have ever played. Doesn´t matter if you´re playing alone or with friends, 7 Days to Die will allways entertain you and give plenty of options to do. It takes a little while to learn and might be a bit frustrating in the beginning but everyone i showed the game got the hang of it. I can only recommend it as it brought me through hard and ♥♥♥♥♥♥ times as my escape from reality. Thank you Devs for this gem of a game

I’ve been having a really great time with 7 Days to Die, and it’s honestly the exact kind of survival game that’s way better with a friend. There is just an insane amount of stuff to do in the world. You can spend your days going on quests, clearing out abandoned buildings, and trying to figure out how to piece together your first actual vehicle. I really like how the progression feels, even if it is a little slow most of the time. Honestly though, that’s how a survival game should be so it keeps you playing for longer. The game gets so intense at night when the zombies suddenly start sprinting at you out of nowhere. The atmosphere gets crazy when you're trapped inside a house in the dark, and you're constantly finding new schematics or better weapon parts that make you want to keep looting just one more place before heading back. The building is awesome too because of the freedom like being able to completely fortify a random rooftop with spikes and fill it with furniture to make it feel like home. My only real complaint is that I wish it was a little easier to find the traders. Other than that, it’s a huge sandbox that keeps you constantly busy, and it's totally worth the time if you want a survival game you can easily sink a ton of hours into.

its definitely fun with friends etc its a good game 100% recommend especially since its cross play. i play here on steam and my friends and brother play on xbox and its cool since i dont like consoles
Classic building game with mood and zombies.  If you like State of Decay series and Project Zomboid this is a spiritual FP Builder/Shooter sibling.

I've been playing this game for over 10 years, put in hundreds more hours than Steam even shows, and I will still probably be playing this game 10 years from now. My friends and I keep coming back to it, building even more stupid bases, getting spooked by random direwolves, and sassed by grumpy traders. 

Pros:
- base building - Our current base looks like a massive coffee percolator that filters zombies in and grinds them up in traps.
- updates - The developers have consistently added more content and changed the way the game works.
- variable stakes - The 7 day zombie horde system keeps the game feeling difficult -> This is an issue that has been fixed in recent updates, as the older version of the game got much much easier. By night 56, it used to be laughably easy. 

Cons:
- Don't expect a story. 
- Prepare to grind.
- Base-building physics is your enemy.

It's a fun game if you want to fight zombies, build an infinite base, dig a tunnel system that spans several in-game miles, and be silly with your friends.

A SCAM PRODUCT -From legit to a scam- A work of passion to a legal ripoff scheme.

Since 7 Days Blood Moon refuses to let me do my review there I will write it here and damn both under the same review. Horror Zombie survival is one of my least favorite subgenres but DAMN is this game addicting. Highly moddable as well, lots of RPG progression systems to keep players clearing POIs looting and scavenging for days. Highly recommended.

It runs on a potato! If you turn on upscaling and turn down settings, this game surprisingly is able to run smooth on just about anything, so if you don't care for good visuals and just want a great survival game, it's a very pleasant surprise in this era of hardware shortages, scalping, and companies pushing AI ♥♥♥♥ that nobody asked for.

As for the gameplay, it's a perfect survival game. Kinda like a 3D but less hardcore version of Zomboid. It leans heavily into crafting though, much deeper than zomboid does, as well as the base building scene is better. It's a lot of fun to play with a bunch of people in co-op.

The balancing is pretty nice, and I feel like this game has a bit of something for everybody. In a large team of like 6-8 players, people will still be able to find their niche roles to contribute to the base's resources with.

Summary:
Bought this masterpiece to play with the boys. We grinded hard on day one, and now they've all magically disappeared into the void. Classic. A fantastic zombie tower defense simulator, assuming your squad doesn't abandon you to survive the apocalypse completely solo.

🎮 Gameplay:
[ ] Just press W
[x] Fun
[ ] Goodbye social life
Opinion: If you are a massive tryhard for tower defense mechanics like I am, this game is a huge W. The core loop is literally just spending six days building a heavily fortified kill-box, only to watch a massive horde of undead completely stress-test your architecture on the seventh day. It's incredibly fun, and obviously way better with friends—assuming yours didn't suddenly get a "real life" after the first session and leave you to micromanage the apocalypse alone.

🎨 Graphics:
[x] Runs on a microwave
[ ] Respectable pixel art
[ ] Melted my GPU
Opinion: Let's be brutally honest: the graphics are objectively hideous. It looks like it was rendered on a toaster from 2012. But honestly? The sheer jankiness somehow adds to the charm and makes the whole desperate survival experience weirdly hilarious. You don't need 4K ray-tracing to bash a zombie's skull in with a spiked club.

🎵 Audio:
[ ] Play while listening to a podcast
[x] Gets the job done
[ ] Mozart is crying in the shower
Opinion: The audio is the absolute definition of "mid." It's not going to win any awards, but it won't make your ears bleed either. It's aggressively average—just enough atmospheric groaning to let you know you're about to get jumped, but not enough to stop you from just blasting your own playlist in the background.

🐛 Bugs:
[ ] It's a feature
[x] Only the funny ones
[ ] Smooth as butter
Opinion: It's got that premium, Bethesda-style jank. There are definitely bugs, but much like Skyrim, they honestly just make the game way more entertaining.

💰 Price:
[ ] Sell a kidney
[x] Wait for a discount
[ ] Worth every penny
Opinion: It's a great time, but don't be a scrub and drop full price on it. Just throw it on your wishlist, go touch grass for a bit, and hold off until GabeN blesses the Steam store with a solid discount.

The only thing that made me sad is when they changed the gear options. Used to be able to really mix and match things (shoulder armor, and torso and legs were separate gear). Even though i takes breaks, i never uninstall this game since i keep coming back to it.

7 Days to Die Review

7 Days to Die stands out from other zombie games like DayZ or Project Zomboid. It mixes survival, crafting, and FPS combat into something unique.

Gameplay & Weapons  
Combat is mostly first‑person (with optional third‑person). Weapons range from primitive bows and clubs to rifles and auto shotguns. Melee can feel clunky, but gunplay is simple and fun, especially against hordes. Explosives like molotovs and pipe bombs add variety.

Survival Systems  
Stamina drains from sprinting, fighting, or jumping, but food and drink restore it. Water must be boiled to avoid dysentery. Food ranges from canned goods to cooked meals, with buffs like faster stamina regen. Infections can occur from zombie bites or dogs, cured with antibiotics or honey. Health is managed with bandages, medkits, and vitamins.

Crafting & Building  
Hundreds of items and blocks can be crafted. Workbenches, forges, and chemistry stations expand options. You can build bases, traps, generators, and decorative structures. Mining and scavenging provide resources like iron, coal, and nitrate.

Armor & Clothing  
Armor pieces can be modded for extra effects. Clothing helps with biome temperatures and sometimes adds protection.

Skills & Levels  
XP comes from killing zombies, looting, and gathering. Leveling grants skill points across categories like strength, agility, and intelligence. Skills have quirky names (e.g., Miner 69’er) and improve efficiency or unlock abilities.

Quests & Traders  
Tutorial quests lead to traders who sell items and give missions (clear zombies, fetch loot, dig treasure). Traders close at night. Currency allows buying/selling, and upgrading trader skills improves their stock.

Customization & Multiplayer  
Character customization is limited but available. Multiplayer supports private servers or communities. Land claims protect bases, and Discord groups host 24/7 servers.

Maps & Biomes  
Maps can be auto‑generated or pre‑made. Biomes include forest, desert, snow, wasteland, and burnt lands. Zombies sprint at night (22:00–4:00), making nighttime dangerous.

Zombies & Animals  
Enemy variety is wide: ferals, cops that spit, zombie dogs, bears, and more. Some attacks feel unfair, but overall they keep things tense.

Creative Mode & Graphics  
Creative mode unlocks everything with no death. Graphics scale well — even on low settings they look decent. Voice chat exists, though text chat is more reliable.

Verdict  
It’s a deep, chaotic, and fun survival game, especially with friends. Despite clunky melee and some quirks, the mix of crafting, combat, and survival makes it stand out. 8/10. I had to shorten this by 4004 characters, so not as detailed as I wanted.
